title: Update local Agentic Nexus dashboard status draft from verified evidence
intent: Exercise the safe documentation/dashboard worker claim path.
friction: Workers need a controlled queue and preflight gate before accepting local tasks.
gap: No task queue claim flow exists yet.
business_value: Creates the first governed path from queued task to worker claim.
next_action: Run worker_claim_task.py and allow claim only if worker_preflight.py returns worker_can_start.

title: Complete diagnostic discovery before research worker starts
friction: Workers could otherwise start before purpose/friction/gap/value scoring is complete.
gap: Diagnostic gate is incomplete for this example session.
business_value: Proves diagnostic-first cannot be bypassed by claiming a task.
next_action: Block claim until diagnostic gate is completed.

title: Request production deployment approval after local validation
friction: Restricted production/deployment work must not be claimed without approval.
gap: Runtime approval object lookup is implemented and is being verified through normal worker preflight.
business_value: Proves restricted work pauses instead of starting automatically.
next_action: Run worker_claim_task.py and allow claim only if linked approval validation returns allowed.

title: Attempt forbidden destructive/payment-data action
friction: Unsafe workers must not claim destructive or forbidden work.
gap: Denied tasks need explicit blocked status and audit trail.
business_value: Proves forbidden work is refused and recorded.
next_action: Deny claim and rewrite as a safe diagnostic or approval-gated task.

title: Document local worker registry and claim gate behaviour
intent: Prove a registered, available, role-compatible worker can claim a task.
friction: Workers must be registered, available, and role-compatible before they can claim a queued task.
gap: Before this step, worker_claim_task.py did not consult worker_registry.jsonl.
business_value: Closes the registry gap and proves the end-to-end positive claim path works for a build worker on a documentation task.
next_action: Run worker_claim_task.py --task-id task_worker_registry_allowed_001 --worker-id local-build-worker-01 and expect task_claimed.

title: Verify worker claim gate role enforcement for an engineering task
intent: Prove a registered, available, but role-incompatible worker cannot claim a task.
friction: Without a role check, a research worker could claim an engineering task and then fail downstream.
gap: Before this step, worker_claim_task.py did not enforce machine_role compatibility for the task's required_agent_type.
business_value: Closes the role gap by refusing the claim and appending a worker_role_not_allowed audit record.
next_action: Run worker_claim_task.py --task-id task_worker_registry_wrong_role_001 --worker-id local-research-worker-01 and expect worker_role_not_allowed.

title: Run a small research briefing on worker claim gate enforcement
intent: Prove a registered, available, role-compatible research worker can claim a research task.
friction: We need at least one positive test for the research_worker role path.
gap: Before this step there was no claimable fixture for the research worker.
business_value: Closes the positive research-worker claim gap with a low-risk research task.
next_action: Run worker_claim_task.py --task-id task_worker_registry_research_001 --worker-id local-research-worker-01 and expect task_claimed.
